hero.subtitle: HydroRacer is a hydrogen racing boat built from scratch by TalTech students for the Monaco Energy Boat Challenge.
mission.label: Mission
mission.statement: To build a centre of excellence in hydrogen technology and deep tech, where students, researchers and companies join forces to drive innovation in green and marine technologies.
team.label: Team
team.text1: Today's technological challenges have grown increasingly complex and require combining knowledge from different fields, practical experience and rapid innovation. Many engineering students lack opportunities to participate in development projects that solve real-world problems, where the knowledge gained through coursework can find a practical outlet. The world increasingly needs deep-tech solutions that support climate neutrality, technological sovereignty and the growth of new high-tech industries. We need a strong new generation of engineers capable of working across disciplines, bridging science with applied engineering and taking ideas from the lab to working solutions.
team.text2: The project creates an environment where young engineers gain hands-on experience in real development processes, collaborate with specialists from different fields and develop technologies whose impact reaches far beyond the university and Estonia.
subteams.label: Team
subteams.title: Five specialised teams
subteams.intro: The project brings together students of electrical power engineering, naval mechanics, product development and business.
subteams.team1.name: ENERGY SYSTEMS
subteams.team1.desc: Responsible for developing and integrating the boat's alternative fuel-based power source. The team's task is to develop a methanol-based solution for onboard electricity generation and ensure efficient and carbon-neutral energy consumption.
subteams.team2.name: ELECTRICAL SYSTEMS
subteams.team2.desc: Responsible for designing the boat's electrical, control and automation systems. The team develops and integrates solutions ensuring the reliability of high and low voltage systems and stable power supply to the boat's critical devices.
subteams.team3.name: HULL & CONSTRUCTION
subteams.team3.desc: Responsible for designing and manufacturing the boat's hull and structural solutions. The team handles material selection, structural strength analysis, manufacturability and durability analysis.
subteams.team4.name: PROPULSION & DYNAMICS
subteams.team4.desc: Responsible for the boat's hydrodynamic behaviour, propulsion solution and hydrofoil development. The team's work includes foil geometry and material strength analysis, and optimisation of the boat's stability and energy efficiency in various conditions.
subteams.team5.name: MARKETING & COMMUNICATIONS
subteams.team5.desc: Responsible for project management, securing funding, establishing partnerships and implementing marketing, while supporting the team through training and team event organisation.
subteams.back.label: Team
monaco.label: Monaco Energy Boat Challenge
monaco.intro: Monaco Energy Boat Challenge is an international student and innovation competition focused on developing sustainable marine technologies and alternative energy solutions. The competition brings together the world's leading universities, engineering teams and maritime industry companies to test and develop next-generation zero-emission propulsion systems and energy-efficient racing boats in real conditions.
monaco.text1: Monaco Energy Boat Challenge is the world's most prestigious zero-emission vessel competition, organised by Yacht Club de Monaco since 2014. Every summer, 40–60 teams from around the world converge on Monaco harbour to prove that clean energy works at sea.
monaco.class0.name: MANOEUVRABILITY
monaco.class0.desc: A low-speed handling challenge focused on precise manoeuvring and operational control within the marina.
monaco.class1.name: ENDURANCE
monaco.class1.desc: Long-duration race in which teams must complete as many laps as possible within the allocated time.
monaco.class2.name: 16 NM SPEED RACE
monaco.class2.desc: Offshore speed race over 16 nautical miles testing long-range performance and reliability.
monaco.text2: The competition is more than sport. It is an international validation platform where new maritime technologies get their first real sea trial. The event is closely watched by European maritime and energy sector investors and industry partners.
timeline.label: Timeline
timeline.title: Road to Monaco
timeline.intro: HydroRacer's development follows a clear phase-by-phase logic. From concept to design, from build to testing — every step brings the team closer to the Monaco Energy Boat Challenge 2027 start line.
timeline.step1.title: Concept
timeline.step1.desc: Team formation, initial concept, feasibility analysis
timeline.step2.title: Design phase
timeline.step3.title: Build phase
timeline.step4.title: Season start
timeline.step5.title: Testing
timeline.step6.title: Monaco Energy Boat Challenge
partners.label: Partners
partners.cta.title: Become our partner
partners.cta.text: We are looking for companies that share our vision of a clean energy future. Benefits of partnership: brand visibility, access to a talented young engineering community, and the opportunity to shape the future of Estonian maritime technology.
